D5 Design L4 Analyze Reference

Background Luminance & Fatigue

comfortable reading isn't maximum contrast — the page should roughly match the room, or the eye keeps re-adapting

Eye strain comes less from contrast than from luminance mismatch. A blazing-white page in a dim room glares; a dim page in a bright room forces the pupil to keep re-adapting between screen and surroundings. The restful target is a background luminance near the ambient level, with contrast kept adequate.

Set the page background luminance and the room. The passage stays readable (text tracks the background), but the comfort gauge peaks only when the page sits near the ambient level — glare at one extreme, adaptation strain at the other.
